title: Klasea dissecta
intro: Klasea dissecta is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Boraginaceae, commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Klasea dissecta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Klasea dissecta is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are the most common herbal preparations made with this plant, although its use and efficacy have not been extensively studied in modern scientific research.
parts: Klasea dissecta is a plant species for which detailed information on medicinal parts is not readily available.
